name: Zinc sensor (ZitR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects zinc, a nutritional and water-quality marker. The native L. lactis ZitR zinc regulator gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the food-grade GRAS chassis Lactococcus lactis.

input.detects: Bioavailable zinc in water, food, or nutritional samples
input.operatingRange: ZitR-based sensing responds across µM zinc

sensing.sensorMechanism: ZitR (a zinc-responsive repressor of the zitSQP operon) binds Zn(II) and changes occupancy of its operator/promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Zn(II) -> ZitR releases PzitS -> transcribes an anti-reporter sgRNA -> dCas9 + sgRNA repress a constitutive reporter (CRISPRi NOT gate). Pair an inverter for turn-on.

parts.name: ZitR regulator
parts.notes: Native zinc-responsive repressor — no porting needed in L. lactis.

performance.limitOfDetection: ZitR module: µM zinc (module-validated in L. lactis).
performance.notes: ZitR is the native L. lactis zinc regulator (no porting); CRISPRi in lactic acid bacteria is established. Integrated device is a design template.

safety.biocontainment: thyA-based biological containment demonstrated in L. lactis; food-grade host.
safety.notes: GRAS / food-grade chassis (no LPS endotoxin); suited to food and nutritional zinc monitoring.

buildSteps.title: Design anti-reporter sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target the reporter promoter; check L. lactis off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: PzitS -> sgRNA (native ZitR). TU2: dCas9 + constitutive mCherry. Use NICE-compatible vectors.

buildSteps.title: Expose to zinc
buildSteps.description: Validate across a zinc standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the native L. lactis ZitR zinc-sensing module with CRISPRi
partsSources: Llull & Poquet / L. lactis zit (ZitR zinc regulation) literature
Bikard et al. 2013, NAR (CRISPRi); CRISPRi in lactic acid bacteria
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
